texto: We can fight fire with water provided we can get it there soon enough. But often we act when it's too late. The result is splattered in the pages of our history: bloodbaths, uprisings, revolutions, you name. And on it goes. We learn so slowly. After so many centuries, we're still a people who eat fire and drink water.' 'Why bother,then?' 'Because we have to believe that one day we'll learn.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Arlene J. Chai que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “We can fight fire with water provided we can get it there soon enough. But often we act when it's…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, revolution, water, com foco em reflexao.

texto: The vision one holds of one's life is so limited, reduced in scope to a moment, so that each person can make choices only within that narrowed reality.

texto: But tales like this must not be taken as truth. You must remind yourself that it is hard to tell where truth ends and a lie begins. So listen all you like, but disbelieve all you hear...You are in the city of lies.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Arlene J. Chai: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “But tales like this must not be taken as truth. You must remind yourself that it is hard to tell …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, lies, propaganda pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e propaganda.

texto: Fate, no doubt, had a hand in it.
explicacao: Citação de Arlene J. Chai sobre temas ligados a fe. “Fate, no doubt, had a hand in it.” articula fe, destiny, faith em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como faith e fate.

texto: I was someone hungry for stories; more specifically, I was someone who craved after facts...I was, you see, at the start of this tale, a person with history. I had no story of my own. Lacking this, I developed a curiosity about other people's lives.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Arlene J. Chai sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “I was someone hungry for stories; more specifically, I was someone who craved after facts...I was…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a vida, identity-crisis, mystery, útil em momentos de mystery.
